title: Prototype
description: How inheritance actually works in JS.

questions.explanation: JS uses prototypal inheritance — each object may link to a parent object.
Object.create sets up prototype linkage. child inherits greet.
Reading walks the prototype chain. Writing creates an OWN property on child.
Arrays inherit from Array.prototype (which inherits from Object.prototype).

questions.prompt: Which does NOT walk the prototype chain?
questions.explanation: Object.keys returns OWN keys only. for-in and `in` see inherited.
Instance's __proto__ === Cat.prototype. meow is found there.

questions.prompt: The topmost prototype for regular objects is:
questions.explanation: Object.prototype's [[Prototype]] is null — end of the chain.
